parameters.jsCode:
const items = $input.all().map(i => i.json);
return items.filter(i => i.ok === true).map(i => ({ json: i }));

const p = $input.item.json; const isDfy = p.tier === 'dfy'; const subject = isDfy ? 'Welcome to the Better Societies DFY EU AI Act Compliance Program' : 'Welcome to your Better Societies EU AI Act Assessment'; const html = isDfy ? `
Better Societies

Welcome, ${p.firstName}.

Your Better Societies DFY EU AI Act Compliance Program is confirmed (€${p.amountTotal.toFixed(0)}) for ${p.company}.

What happens next:
  1. Today: We confirm scope on a 30-minute kickoff call
  2. Week 1: Full AI-systems inventory + Annex III classification
  3. Week 2-4: Compliance documentation + remediation plan built and delivered
  4. Ongoing: Managed compliance updates through the Aug 2, 2026 deadline

Book your kickoff call

Adrien
Better Societies · bettersocieties.world

` : `
Better Societies

Welcome, ${p.firstName}.

Your EU AI Act compliance assessment is confirmed (€${p.amountTotal.toFixed(0)}).

What happens next:
  1. We review your AI systems and build your initial compliance snapshot
  2. Your EU AI Act readiness report arrives within 48 hours
  3. We schedule your 30-minute review call to walk through findings
  4. You leave with a prioritized action list for the Aug 2, 2026 deadline
Where clients usually go after their assessment:
  • Gap Assessment (€2,500). We review your current documentation and processes against every article that applies to you and give you a prioritised fix list.
  • Compliance Roadmap (€5,000). You get a complete implementation plan, including templates for your technical documentation, risk management, and human oversight policies.
  • Done-For-You Full Conformity (€25,000). We do the entire job for you, from AI system registration to your Declaration of Conformity, with 30 days of questions answered afterwards. Every euro you have already paid is credited.

Questions before your report arrives? Reply directly to this email.

Adrien
Better Societies · bettersocieties.world

`; return [{ json: { ...p, subject, html } }];

const items = $input.all().map(i => i.json);
return items.filter(i => i.ok !== true).map(i => ({ json: i }));
